qa_scope: strict control-map artifact QA for local OpenPose, DepthAnything, Lineart, and BAE Normal preprocessor outputs
certification_scope: local control-map preprocessor readiness and artifact QA only

dependency_recovery.issue: BAE normal preprocessor missing timm dependency
dependency_recovery.fix: Installed timm into the local ComfyUI venv and reran only BAE-NormalMapPreprocessor.

visual_findings: Black-background pose map was generated with visible colored body joints and limb connections.
Face and hand landmark dots are visible around the head/upper body region.
The source is a cropped portrait, so lower-body pose coverage is limited and not suitable as a full-body pose certification map.
Grayscale depth map was generated with a coherent portrait foreground separated from a darker background.
Head, hair mass, shoulders, and jacket form are readable as spatial layers.
Facial features are intentionally smoothed by the depth model; this is acceptable for depth structure but not identity detail.
Lineart map was generated with strong hair, eye, face, collar, and jacket contours.
The map has dense facial and hair detail useful for contour QA.
The lineart is high contrast and somewhat dense, so future generation use should start at low to moderate strength.
Normal map was generated after installing the missing timm dependency.
The output uses expected normal-map color bands and preserves face, hair, collar, and shoulder form.
The result is smoothed as expected for a normal estimator and should be QA-tested again before any generation lane uses it.

remaining_risks: These are preprocessor artifacts only; no OpenPose, depth, normal, or lineart ControlNet generation model was installed or proven.
The source image is a cropped portrait, so OpenPose is not a full-body pose certification sample.

Future generation lanes need matching SDXL ControlNet model registry records, path/hash proof, static validation, bounded generation, pullback, technical QA, and whole-image visual QA before promotion.
next_action: Use these artifacts as local preprocessor readiness evidence; next non-EC2 task is model-registry planning/provisioning for one selected SDXL pose/depth/normal/lineart ControlNet lane or a dedicated QA sheet exporter.
